site.name: Tanvir Ahmed
site.headline: I build end-to-end web applications UI, APIs, and databases with performance and clean UX in mind.
about: I am a Full-Stack Developer in Dhaka, building end-to-end web apps with JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Next.js, Node.js, and MongoDB. I focus on clean UI, reliable APIs, and products that feel fast and easy to use.

I have delivered work such as the Luxxas E-commerce platform (frontend and backend), SplasBD and Panzo — full-stack Bangladeshi fashion e-commerce platforms — and SchdoSocial, a Facebook Page post scheduling SaaS I built end to end. I am open to freelance, internships, and full-time opportunities where I can grow while shipping useful software.

projects.title: Luxxas E-commerce — Frontend (Client Project)
projects.description: A modern e-commerce frontend built with Next.js featuring responsive UI, product discovery, and real-time cart management for a seamless shopping experience.
projects.fullDescription:
Overview

Luxxas is a client e-commerce platform where I owned the frontend experience end to end — from product discovery to checkout-ready cart flows.

What I built

  • Responsive storefront with category browsing and product detail pages
  • Cart and wishlist flows with optimistic UI updates
  • Reusable UI components styled with Tailwind CSS and Next UI
  • Data fetching and caching with TanStack Query

Outcome

The result is a fast, mobile-friendly shopping experience that keeps navigation simple and product discovery clear for everyday users.

details.label: Stack
details.value: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query

projects.title: Luxxas E-commerce — Backend (Client Project)
projects.description: Production-grade REST API powering catalog, cart, checkout, orders, and user management with JWT auth, OAuth, role-based access, and strong validation/security.
projects.fullDescription:
Overview

I built the backend API layer for Luxxas to support catalog management, cart/checkout, orders, and secure user accounts.

Key features

  • REST API design with Express.js and TypeScript
  • JWT authentication with role-based access control
  • MongoDB data models with Mongoose
  • Input validation, file uploads, and email notifications

Security & reliability

The API uses structured validation, protected routes, and clear error handling so the frontend can rely on predictable responses in production.

projects.title: SplasBD — Full-Stack E-commerce Platform
projects.description: A production-ready Bangladesh-focused B2C e-commerce platform with Next.js storefront, Express API, admin panel, COD checkout, Steadfast shipping, promo codes, reviews, and SEO.
projects.fullDescription:
Overview

SplasBD is a Bangladesh-centric full-stack e-commerce monorepo (client/ + server/) where customers browse products, manage cart, checkout as guest or registered users, and track orders — while admins run the entire store from a dedicated dashboard.

Customer Storefront

  • Homepage with hero carousel, category showcase, promo sections, and trust blocks
  • Product listing with filters (category, price, brand, rating, gender, size, color), sort, and pagination
  • Product detail pages with image gallery, size/color selection, size chart, and verified reviews
  • Search with autocomplete and trending queries
  • localStorage cart with Bangladeshi address (division, district, upazila)
  • Guest and authenticated checkout with promo codes and shipping charge calculation (COD)
  • Order confirmation, guest order lookup, and account linking on login
  • Email/password auth, Google OAuth, email verification, and password reset
  • Dynamic sitemap, robots.txt, and SSR product metadata for SEO

Admin Panel

  • Real-time dashboard with order polling and revenue stats
  • Product CRUD with up to 30 Cloudinary images, sizes, colors, stock, and slugs
  • Category and subcategory management with homepage display order
  • Order management with status workflow and Steadfast courier shipment
  • User and role management, banners, promo codes, and promotional sections
  • Shipping settings, size charts (BD/UK/US), analytics, and site settings
  • CMS editors for Terms, Privacy, and About pages (TipTap rich text)

Backend & Architecture

  • Express 5 + TypeScript with 17 feature-based modules (auth, products, orders, cart, promos, reviews, search…)
  • MongoDB + Mongoose ODM with JWT access/refresh tokens (HTTP-only cookies)
  • Cloudinary image uploads, Gmail SMTP emails, Steadfast/Packzy courier API + webhooks
  • Security: Helmet, CORS, bcrypt password hashing, role-based access control
  • Deployed on Vercel (client + serverless API); Render config available for alternative hosting

projects.title: Panzo — Full-Stack Fashion E-commerce Platform
projects.description: A production-grade Bangladesh-focused fashion e-commerce ecosystem with Next.js storefront, Vite admin panel, Express API, RBAC, expense/investment tracking, custom recommendation engine, and analytics.
projects.fullDescription:
Overview

Panzo Online Shopping (Tanvir E-commerce) is a production-grade, Bangladesh-focused fashion e-commerce ecosystem with three apps — client/ (Next.js 16 storefront), admin/ (Vite + React management panel), and server/ (Express API) — sharing one REST backend and MongoDB.

Architecture

  • Client (port 3000) and Admin (port 5173) both talk to Express API at /api/v1
  • MongoDB for commerce data + separate activity-log database for audit trail
  • JWT access/refresh tokens in HTTP-only cookies; Passport Google & Facebook OAuth
  • Cloudinary CDN for product media; Nodemailer for verification and password reset
  • Vercel-ready CORS for .vercel.app preview deployments

Client Storefront

  • Home carousel, category/brand browsing, product detail with color × size variants and Facebook Reel embed
  • ShopProvider React Context — cart, wishlist, user, addresses with localStorage persistence
  • Bangladesh checkout: division → district → upazila cascade, Dhaka ৳60 / outside ৳120 shipping, BDT currency
  • Guest checkout, search with autocomplete, infinite scroll product lists
  • SEO: dynamic metadata, sitemap, robots, JSON-LD structured data
  • Client-side analytics tracking feeds the recommendation algorithm

Admin Panel

  • RBAC with 6+ roles (admin, moderator, super_user, reseller, product_manager, promoter)
  • Module permissions: dashboard, users, products, categories, brands, orders, banners, expenses, investments, analytics
  • Dashboard with revenue, stock, expense, and investment summaries + Recharts
  • Multi-variant product CRUD (color × size × stock × per-size pricing), Cloudinary uploads
  • Custom manual orders, expense distribution, promoter product views, jsPDF reports

Server & Business Logic

  • 14 feature modules: user, product, category, brand, banner, order, expense, investment, dashboard, permission, rbac, tracking, analytics, upload
  • Order lifecycle: pending → confirmed → processing → shipped → delivered / cancelled
  • COD + online payment enums; multi-variant product model with sourcing cost and specs
  • Custom recommendation engine — weighted scoring (interest 45%, popularity 20%, new 20%, random 15%) with 10-min cache
  • Security: Helmet, CORS, rate limiting, express-mongo-sanitize, Zod + express-validator

Outcomes

  • Complete SME e-commerce solution — storefront, operations, finance, and analytics in one stack
  • Bangladesh-ready UX: address hierarchy, COD-first checkout, BDT pricing
  • Enterprise admin with RBAC, expense/investment tracking, and behavior-based recommendations

projects.title: SchdoSocial — Facebook Post Scheduling & Auto-Publishing SaaS
projects.description: A full-stack SaaS for Facebook Page managers to connect multiple Pages, compose posts, schedule recurring content, and auto-publish via Agenda job queue with real-time SSE notifications, admin panel, and Meta compliance.
projects.fullDescription:
Overview

SchdoSocial (AutoPost) is a production full-stack SaaS monorepo (Client/ + Server/) that solves manual Facebook Page posting for marketers and small businesses. Users connect Pages from one dashboard, compose and preview posts, schedule one-off or recurring content, and rely on background workers to publish automatically at the right time — with success/failure alerts in real time.

Problem & Solution

  • Manual posting across multiple Pages is time-consuming and error-prone
  • SchdoSocial centralizes Page management, media uploads, scheduling models, and automated publishing
  • Recurring scheduling rules (daily/weekly/monthly) with time windows and page staggering
  • Reliable publish pipeline with Agenda jobs + poll-worker fallback, atomic claims, and retry on failure

Client — Next.js 16 Dashboard

  • Landing page with SEO (sitemap, robots, Open Graph, JSON-LD FAQ schema) and blog content
  • Auth: email/password, Google OAuth, Facebook OAuth + separate Pages connect flow
  • Onboarding wizard guiding new users from Page connect to first scheduled post
  • Post composer: text, images/video, hashtags, place tags, post types (post/reel/image/story)
  • Calendar view for scheduled posts, scheduling models for recurring rules, activity log
  • Real-time notifications via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
  • Super-admin panel: users, posts, Pages, analytics, audit log, worker status, Cloudinary pools
  • English + Bengali i18n; legal pages (Privacy v1.1, Terms v1.1, data deletion for Meta review)

Server — Express 5 API

  • REST API at /api/v1: auth, facebook-pages, posts, uploads, notifications, scheduling-models, activity, legal, admin, health
  • MongoDB + Mongoose: users, authtokens, facebookconnections, facebookpages, posts, schedulingmodels, notifications, cloudinarypools, useractivitylogs
  • Passport (local, Google, Facebook) with JWT in HttpOnly cookies; automatic token refresh from Client
  • Cloudinary media (production) with admin-managed storage pools; local uploads in dev
  • Facebook Graph API integration with AES-256-GCM encrypted Page tokens
  • Security: Helmet, rate limiting (global/auth/password/upload), strict CORS, trust proxy for Coolify/nginx

Auto-Publishing Pipeline

  • Primary: Agenda job queue schedules publish at scheduledAt; worker executes Facebook Graph publish
  • Fallback: Poll worker every 15s with atomic publishClaimedAt claims — supports 500+ concurrent scheduled publishes
  • Same-page publish gap to respect rate limits; post lifecycle: draft → scheduled → published / failed (retry)
  • SSE hub pushes publish success/fail instantly; optional SMTP email notifications

Privacy & Meta Compliance

  • Meta deauthorize + data-deletion signed_request callbacks
  • In-app Facebook data deletion and full account purge endpoints
  • Public legal pages indexed for Meta Platform review requirements

Deployment

  • Separate Client (port 3000) and Server (port 5000) apps on Coolify
  • Live at schdosocial.tech with API at server.schdosocial.tech

Outcomes

  • Eliminates manual daily posting; multi-Page management from one account
  • 500+ concurrent scheduled publish support with claim + retry reliability
  • Full compliance stack: Privacy/Terms v1.1, Meta data deletion, SEO-ready public pages
